title: Cardiac arrest
summary: Unresponsive, not breathing normally — start CPR immediately.
actions: Call for backup + AED immediately
Start compressions: 100–120/min, 5–6 cm deep, full recoil
30:2 with ventilations, minimize pauses
Attach AED as soon as it arrives — follow prompts
Rotate compressors every 2 min
redFlags: Any delay to compressions reduces survival ~10% per minute

title: Heart attack (MI)
summary: Crushing chest pain/pressure, often with sweating and nausea.
actions: Stop all exertion, sit patient down (W-position)
Call ambulance for transport — this is time-critical
Aspirin 300 mg chewed (if no allergy/bleeding)
Monitor closely — be ready for arrest (AED nearby)

title: Stroke
summary: FAST: Face droop, Arm weakness, Speech trouble — Time to evacuate.
signs: Sudden severe headache or vision loss
actions: Note exact time of symptom onset — critical for thrombolysis
Immediate ambulance transport, no delays on scene
Nothing by mouth (swallowing may be impaired)
Position: slightly elevated head if conscious; recovery position if not

title: Hypoglycemia
summary: Diabetic or exhausted athlete: shaky, sweaty, confused — give sugar.
actions: Conscious & can swallow: 15–20 g fast sugar (gel, juice, cola)
Re-check in 10–15 min, repeat sugar if still symptomatic
Follow with slow carbs (bar, sandwich) once improving
Unconscious: recovery position, NOTHING by mouth, call ambulance (IM glucagon if available)

title: Heat stroke
summary: Hot + confused athlete = emergency. Cool first, transport second.
signs: Hot skin — sweating may continue in exertional heat stroke
actions: Move to shade, strip excess clothing
Aggressive cooling NOW: ice-water immersion if available, else ice packs to neck/armpits/groin + douse with water and fan
Cool first, transport second (target <39°C)
Monitor airway and consciousness throughout

title: Heat exhaustion
summary: Dizzy, nauseous, heavy sweating but mentally intact — rest and cool.
signs: Normal or mildly elevated mentation — answers correctly
actions: Stop activity, shade, lie down with legs elevated
Cool with water spray + fanning, cold packs
Oral rehydration with electrolytes if not vomiting
Reassess in 15–30 min before any return to course
redFlags: Any confusion → treat as heat stroke

title: Anaphylaxis
summary: Rapid allergic reaction with airway/breathing/circulation involvement — adrenaline first.
actions: IM adrenaline 0.5 mg anterolateral thigh IMMEDIATELY (EpiPen)
Call ambulance — always transport after adrenaline
Lie flat, legs raised (sitting up only if breathing is hard)
Repeat adrenaline after 5 min if no improvement
redFlags: Collapse — never stand the patient up suddenly

title: Asthma attack
summary: Wheezing, struggling to speak — assist with their inhaler, sit upright.
actions: Sit upright, stay calm and coach slow breathing
4–10 puffs salbutamol via spacer, repeat every 10–15 min as needed
No improvement after 10 puffs → ambulance
Monitor for exhaustion — quieting wheeze can be deterioration

title: Seizure
summary: Protect from injury, time the seizure, recovery position after.
actions: Time it from the start; clear hard objects away, pad the head
Do NOT restrain, nothing in the mouth
When jerking stops: airway check + recovery position
Stay until fully reoriented; check glucose if possible
redFlags: >5 min or repeated seizures (status) → ambulance + escalate
Seizure after head injury or in water

title: Severe bleeding
summary: Direct pressure first, tourniquet for limb bleeds that won't stop.
actions: Gloves on; firm direct pressure with dressing
Bleeding through? Add a second dressing on top, press harder
Uncontrollable limb bleed: tourniquet 5–7 cm above the wound, note the time
Treat for shock: lie flat, keep warm; urgent transport

title: Fractured femur
summary: Shortened, rotated, very painful thigh — splint, treat for shock, transport.
signs: Severe thigh pain, unable to weight-bear
actions: Don't move until splinted; check pulse/sensation below injury
Traction splint if trained & available, else immobilize to the other leg with padding
Treat for shock — femur can lose 1–1.5 L blood internally
Analgesia per protocol; ambulance transport

title: Ankle / lower limb injury
summary: Twisted ankle: RICE; suspect fracture if bony tenderness or can't walk 4 steps.
actions: Rest, Ice (max 20 min), Compression bandage, Elevation
Bony tenderness or can't weight-bear → immobilize, treat as fracture
Check pulse/sensation distal to the injury
Arrange transport off course — no hopping kilometres

title: Head injury / concussion
summary: Any knocked head with confusion or LOC comes off the course — period.
actions: Remove from activity immediately — no same-day return
Assess responsiveness (AVPU) and reassess every few minutes
Suspect neck injury with high-energy mechanism — keep head still
Worsening symptoms → ambulance for CT

title: Spinal injury (suspected)
summary: High-energy mechanism + neck/back pain or neuro signs — don't move them.
actions: Tell the patient to keep still; hold head in neutral manual stabilization
Move only for airway problems or scene danger (log-roll with help)
Keep warm, monitor breathing
Ambulance with immobilization equipment
redFlags: Altered consciousness — airway takes priority over the spine

title: Hyponatremia (overhydration)
summary: Endurance athlete who drank lots of plain water: bloated, confused — do NOT give more fluids.
signs: High plain-water intake during long events
Headache, nausea, confusion — can mimic dehydration
actions: STOP all hypotonic fluids — no water, no sports drink
Mild + alert: salty snacks, observe
Confusion/vomiting → urgent transport (needs hypertonic saline)
Document fluid intake history for hospital
redFlags: Treating as dehydration and giving fluids makes it fatal

title: Hypothermia
summary: Cold, wet, slowing down: insulate from the ground, rewarm gently, handle gently.
signs: Cold exposure: wet clothing, wind, exhaustion
actions: Shelter from wind/rain; remove wet clothing
Insulate from ground; wrap including head (hypothermia wrap)
Conscious + shivering: warm sweet drinks, calorie intake
Severe (no shivering, drowsy): handle very gently, no standing/walking, urgent evacuation

title: Shock
summary: Pale, clammy, fast weak pulse — find and treat the cause, lie flat, keep warm.
actions: Lie flat, elevate legs (unless breathing problems/chest injury)
Control any external bleeding
Keep warm, nothing by mouth
Urgent transport — monitor consciousness and pulse continuously

title: Choking
summary: Can't speak or cough: 5 back blows, 5 abdominal thrusts, repeat.
actions: Effective cough → encourage coughing, watch
Ineffective: 5 sharp back blows between shoulder blades
Then 5 abdominal thrusts; alternate 5-and-5
Unconscious → start CPR, look in mouth before breaths

title: Dehydration
summary: Thirst, dark urine, fatigue and dizziness — rehydrate gradually.
actions: Move to shade, stop activity, sit/lie down
Sip oral rehydration / electrolyte fluids gradually
Cool the skin; loosen clothing
Recheck in 15–20 min; escalate if not improving

title: Exercise-associated muscle cramps
summary: Sudden painful muscle spasm — stretch, hydrate, replace salts.
actions: Gently stretch and hold the affected muscle
Light massage once the spasm eases
Rehydrate with electrolyte fluids
Rest before returning to activity

title: Fainting (vasovagal syncope)
summary: Brief LOC with quick recovery — lay flat, raise the legs.
actions: Lay flat, raise legs ~30 cm
Loosen tight clothing, ensure fresh air
Keep down until fully recovered, then sit slowly
Give fluids once alert; find/treat the cause
redFlags: Injury from the fall / head strike

title: Hyperventilation / panic attack
summary: Fast breathing, tingling, anxiety — calm and slow the breathing.
actions: Reassure calmly; move away from the crowd
Coach slow breathing (in 4s, out 6s)
Rule out asthma/cardiac/allergic cause first
Stay until breathing and pulse settle
redFlags: Blue lips, true shortness of breath

title: Nosebleed (epistaxis)
summary: Pinch the soft nose, lean forward, 10–15 min.
signs: Often after a knock or in dry heat
actions: Sit up, head tipped slightly forward
Pinch the soft part of the nose firmly for 10–15 min
Breathe through the mouth; don't release early
Apply a cold pack to the bridge of the nose

title: Sprain / strain
summary: Joint/muscle injury — rest, ice, compression, elevation.
signs: Pain, swelling, bruising around a joint/muscle
actions: Rest — stop activity, protect the limb
Ice 15–20 min (cloth between ice and skin)
Compress with a bandage (not too tight)
Elevate above heart level; review weight-bearing
redFlags: Cannot bear weight / use the limb
Numb, cold or blue beyond the injury

title: Dislocated shoulder
summary: Arm held away from body, squared-off shoulder — immobilize, don't force.
signs: Arm held slightly out, resists movement
actions: Support the arm in the position found
Do NOT attempt to relocate it in the field
Pad and sling/immobilize; cold pack for pain
Arrange transport for reduction + X-ray
redFlags: Numb/cold hand, weak pulse at wrist

title: Eye injury / foreign body
summary: Irritation or trauma to the eye — flush, don't rub, cover.
signs: Sensation of something in the eye
actions: Loose grit/chemical: flush with clean water/saline 10+ min
Don't rub; don't remove an embedded object
Cover both eyes lightly to rest movement
Refer for chemical, penetrating, or vision-loss injuries

title: Sunburn
summary: Red, hot, sore skin from sun — cool, hydrate, cover.
actions: Get out of the sun, cool the skin (cool water/compress)
Hydrate; give simple analgesia if appropriate
Cover with light clothing; don't burst blisters
Aftersun / aloe for comfort

title: Insect sting / bite
summary: Local pain/swelling — remove sting, cool; watch for allergy.
actions: Scrape out a bee sting (don't squeeze the sac)
Wash the area; apply a cold pack
Antihistamine for itch if available/appropriate
Watch 15–30 min for spreading/allergic signs
redFlags: Facial/lip/tongue swelling or wheeze → treat as anaphylaxis

title: Diabetic high blood sugar (hyperglycemia)
summary: Thirst, drowsiness, fruity breath in a diabetic — needs medical care.
actions: Do NOT give sugar (opposite of a hypo)
Encourage water if alert and able to swallow
Let them take their own insulin if they can
Arrange urgent transport — can deteriorate fast

title: Abdominal pain / side stitch
summary: Most stitches are benign — but screen for serious abdominal pain.
signs: Stitch: sharp upper-side pain on exertion, eases with rest
actions: Stitch: slow down, deep breathe, stretch the side, press the area
Rest and rehydrate
Serious pain: keep nil by mouth, position of comfort
Escalate persistent, severe, or localized pain

title: Frostbite / cold injury
summary: Numb, white/waxy skin on extremities — rewarm gently, don't rub.
actions: Get into shelter/warmth; remove wet/tight items
Rewarm gently with body heat or warm (not hot) water
Do NOT rub the area or use direct heat
Don't rewarm if refreezing is possible; protect and transport

title: Knocked-out tooth (dental)
summary: Handle by the crown, keep moist, see a dentist fast.
signs: Tooth fully out of socket after impact
actions: Hold the tooth by the crown, never the root
If clean, reinsert gently; otherwise store in milk/saliva
Bite on gauze to control bleeding
Seek dental care within ~30–60 min for best result

title: Minor wound / laceration
summary: Clean, control bleeding, dress — decide if it needs closure.
signs: Break in the skin with minor bleeding
actions: Direct pressure to stop bleeding
Clean with water/saline once bleeding controlled
Cover with a clean non-stick dressing
Refer for stitches if deep, gaping, or over a joint
redFlags: Spurting/uncontrolled bleeding → treat as severe bleeding
